item.title: Why peaceful protests do more than you think, and are far more successful than violent protests.
item.description: https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20190513-it-only-takes-35-of-people-to-change-the-world In 1986, millions of Filipinos took to the streets of Manila in peaceful protest and prayer in the People Power movement. The Marcos regime folded on the fourth day. In 2003, the people of Georgia ousted Eduard Shevardnadze through the bloodless Rose Revolution, in which protestors stormed the parliament building holding the flowers in their hands. While in 2019, the presidents of Sudan and Algeria both announced they would step aside after decades in office, thanks to peaceful campaigns of resistance. In each case, civil resistance by ordinary members of the public trumped the political elite to achieve radical change. There are, of course, many ethical reasons to use nonviolent strategies. But compelling research by Erica Chenoweth, a political scientist at Harvard University, confirms that civil disobedience is not only the moral choice; it is also the most powerful way of shaping world politics – by a long way. Looking at hundreds of campaigns over the last century, Chenoweth found that nonviolent campaigns are twice as likely to achieve their goals as violent campaigns. And although the exact dynamics will depend on many factors, she has shown it takes around 3.5% of the population actively participating in the protests to ensure serious political change. Everyone has a part to play. Simply canceling your subscriptions to the billionaire class with the sorry to see you go message of "Cancel Donald Trump #RemoveTheKing" could play great role in obtaining actionable change. One thing is for sure, he releases his cronies from prison to show the world if you do his bidding, you won't suffer consequences. After Jan. 6th, and his continued trolling that he wants to stay in office come re-election, everyone should be advocating for his removal in order to protect America and the American people. Our Constitution doesn't protect us so much as we must protect it from those who abandon it for their own power, for then we could lose the Constitution entirely. https://i.imgur.com/O24msCd.png Fascism is the measles of mankind. - Albert Einstein Many people have many opinions on how we got here. There are many underlying causes, from socioeconomic causes rooted in race, on both sides of the isle. If you think white rural men are falling in line to Donald Trump for any other reason than they feel left behind, which they have been, you are mostly, not entirely, wrong. The only way forward is to solve problems for those most facing problems, those people are poor people, and the middle class is following. Those people are people from all walks of life, from all racial backgrounds, all kinds of jobs, and educations. If we want to bring up people in urban America, we must also bring up people in rural America. If we want to bring up people in rural America, then we also must bring up people in urban America. People deserve decent educations, and better opportunity in every community in America. If one group lacks, all groups are affected. When one group gains too much, it takes away from other groups. The group that has taken too much are the billionaires. They manipulate money for nothing but their own benefit, where it may be beneficial to buy and run a competitive successful business into the ground. Use their social media presence to manipulate stock prices. Where it may be beneficial to just outright buy the government, write their own laws, and have their representatives pass them near blindly. We live in a system designed for two parties. For many positions, in many locations, it is important for people to run in a viable party. Until we can tweak our system of elections to make them more fair, ranked voting or such, we must operate with what we have. Get involved. We currently allow about 11% of the nation to pick their candidate for presidency. This stat is based on the fact roughly 23% of voting Americans participate in primary elections. One side of those 23%, are the people who pick the candidate that becomes president. This allows candidates to be chosen that the majority of people do not want president, at all, including Donald Trump. In fact, the candidate disliked by most American's was the candidate that won, twice. It started with people not participating in primary elections, thinking just their vote or no-vote in the general election would be, enough, and a decent use of their civil duty. The parties are less immune to you joining them, and advocating for your change that they would ever wish you to realize. You can change a political party in a few months. If you can figure out how to transfer protest power to voter outreach power, you can win America, and obtain the laws and regulations you desire. The voting primaries you desire, and not this convoluted mess designed to keep you from participating. It is time to take initiative. You need to research how your local primaries are operated. You need to research how your local parties are operated. If you understand these things, you need to reach other people wanting and willing to learn. Sometimes these processes are designed to keep you from participating, over come it. Be strong. If someone is advocating against Donald Trump, don't make it your goal to argue with them. If you feel what is happening isn't enough, promote that change positively, you are correct. More needs to be done. Be positive. Congratulate people on their peaceful efforts to stop fascism. Encourage and organize longer protesting. I think Donald Trump can be defeated easily, and the only way he'll ever become a full blown three plus term fascist is if we simply allow it.
item.dc:creator: EnIightenment

item.title: GovBrief Today #262
item.description: ? Donald Trump's reaction to seven million of us in the streets protesting his administration was to post an AI video of himself wearing a crown and dumping feces on the protesters. Bullying, humiliating, and dehumanizing have been his MO since he announced his first campaign a decade ago and called Mexican immigrants rapists and criminals. He treats people as pawns. And if they don't behave that way, he'll make them. We learned today that Trump and Rubio abandoned FBI gang informants to secure access to El Salvador's torture prison. And he told the nation today that he could use the Insurrection Act to give himself "unquestioned power”, something dictators say, not presidents. We need to stop this now. Showing the size of the movement against him was a great warning, but we have to follow up with local work this week. Contact the people and organizations who led your local rallies and begin working with them because there are more than enough of us to bring about change if we stay committed. Don't let algorithms curate your reality. Every night: raw evidence from local and national sources nationwide - images, data, quotes you can verify yourself in under 3 minutes. Free and private at govbrief.today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-airport-hunting-stand [2] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-beef-argentina-purchase [3] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-colombia-aid-petro [4] https://go.govbrief.today/rubio-el-salvador-informants [5] https://go.govbrief.today/shrapnel-hits-motorcade [6] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-sf-guard-deployment [7] https://go.govbrief.today/santos-prison-reform-vow [8] https://go.govbrief.today/senator-justice-tax-lien [9] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-video-dump-feces

item.title: Ok I laughed
item.description:
Yeah yeah I know, we shouldn’t make fun of things outside of their control. But I can suspend that rule once in a while for Stephen Miller.
Bonus Stephen Miller meme that I could never post because I can’t think of a good title (and it’s an OK meme, not great, not bad)
